revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神]]
{{infobox deity| member_of= {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|lt=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|lt=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|simple|User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}} | type = Shinto | not_children = | not_father = | not_consort = | not_mother = |ignore = is one of the {{ill|Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|lt=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|lt=Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|simple|User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|ja|十七世神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seventeen Generations of Earthly Deities|qq=}}. This is a group of Japanese deities ({{ill|Kunitsukami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=}}) that are all in a genealogical relationship descending from {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|建速須佐之男命|simple|goodbye $1/Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}.goodbye $1 | Name = Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami | Divine Order = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full Name = 日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神 (Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami) | Alternate Name= 日名照額田毗道男伊許知迩神 | Other Names = | Divine Rank = | Divine Role = Agricultural deity | Mausoleum = | father = Possibly Hinaterinukatabichio no Kami | mother = | Relatives = | consort = {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}} | children = {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}} | cult_centre = | Texts = {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} | gender= {{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}} | offspring= {{ill|Q48759713|qid=Q48759713|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami}} }}
'''Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami''' (日名照額田毘道男伊許知邇神, ヒナテリヌカタビチオイコチニ, {{smaller|Historical kana orthography:}} ヒナテリヌカタビチヲイコチニ) is a female deity appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.
== Overview == There are variations in the pronunciation of the deity's name across texts, with some rendering it as ''Hinaterinu...'' and others as ''Hinateriru...''.
This deity appears exclusively in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', but details beyond her genealogy are unclear.
The long name can be divided into "Hinateru," "Nukatabimichi," and "Okichini." The name "Hinateru Nukatabimichi" likely refers to her father, while "Okichini" is her own name.{{Sfn|Nishinomiya|2014|pp=381-382}} * '''Hinateri''': "Sunlit" or "Shining under the sun." * '''Nukada''': "Square-shaped fields" or fertile lands, with the implication of good harvests. * '''Bichio''': A "man traveling the path to Nukata fields." * '''Ikochini''': Represents "flourishing spirit" or "bountiful rice harvest," referring to her role as an agricultural deity.
From this etymology, her name can be interpreted as "The flourishing spirit born to the sunlit Nukata path." Her agricultural significance is thought to have influenced her son, {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Ohokuninushi family tree.png|right|thumb|250px|Genealogy of Ōkuninushi (as per the ''Kojiki''). Blue represents male deities, red represents female deities, and yellow represents deities of unknown gender.]]
Hinaterinukatabichioikochini no Kami is married to {{ill|Torinarumi-no-Kami|ja|鳥鳴海神|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|1=Torinarumi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Torinarumi-no-Kami|qq=}}, the child of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}. Together, they bore {{ill|Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|ja|国忍富神|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|1=Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninotoshimi-no-Kami|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{reflist}}
=== Sources === {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書 |title=古事記 |volume= |date=October 30, 2014 |publisher={{ill|Shinchosha|en|Shinchosha|qid=Q1184979|lt=Shinchosha|1=Shinchosha}} |ref={{SfnRef|Nishinomiya|2014}} |author={{ill|Kazutami Nishinomiya|ja|西宮一民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazutami Nishinomiya|1=Kazutami Nishimiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11627607}} (Editor) |isbn=978-4-10-620801-0 |series={{ill|Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium|ja|新潮日本古典集成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium|1=Shincho Japanese Classical Compendium|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503629}} (New Edition)}}
